steps.title: Define the universal pattern
steps.content: The supposed universal tendency is that cultures map time onto imagined linear axes, such as opposite directions for past and future. Morales and colleagues claim that tendency is not universal.

steps.title: Look for a Yupno exception
steps.content: A concerns which hand is used, C concerns grammar shared with Hebrew, and D concerns indoor versus outdoor gestures. Evidence that future and past gestures are not opposite linear directions directly challenges the proposed universal pattern. The answer is B.
